notes: SHARED-REDIS / NOISY-TENANT TRAP. The nightly cache-audit cron (scheduled near onset) runs 'KEYS *' followed by a bulk DEL against the shared Redis primary session-cache-01. KEYS * scans the entire keyspace and blocks the single-threaded Redis event loop; the bulk DEL then drives an eviction storm (maxmemory) and a blocked_clients spike. Three UNRELATED services that all use session-cache-01 -- auth (session tokens), cart (cart state), rate-limiter (counters) -- have their reads/writes time out at the same moment. They share NO service-call edge in service_map.json: they sit in different call graphs and only co-depend on the Redis primary. auth is LOUDEST/paged (login failures). Control services catalog (own Postgres datastore) and api-gateway do NOT touch session-cache-01 and stay healthy, proving the fault is the shared Redis, not a global outage. Origin is the Redis primary saturation; cache-audit is the heavy tenant whose access pattern triggered it.
_design_note: Paged on auth because login failures are the most user-visible symptom. auth is one of three services whose Redis calls timed out simultaneously; check WHY (the shared Redis primary blocked_clients/eviction spike), not just auth's own logs.
